page: Amazon Web Services h1.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. Running on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture system features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz and includes 45 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance is bundled with 8000 GB of local HDD storage, positioning it for high-capacity local data needs. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the h1.8xlarge exhibits average multi-core CPU performance and average memory read bandwidth. However, single-core performance is poor, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks and single-threaded compression tasks are also weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the h1.8xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage relative to its compute resources, making it a practical option for workloads requiring large-scale sequential data access without the premium cost of solid-state drives. Given its average multi-core capabilities and massive local storage, this instance is suited for data warehousing, map-reduce jobs, distributed file systems, and large-scale log processing where high capacity is prioritized over rapid single-threaded execution.
description: Amazon Web Services h1.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server designed for high-capacity local storage workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores, this x86_64 system includes 128.0 GB of RAM and 8000 GB of local HDD storage. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU and memory read performance, though single-core processing and memory write speeds are weak. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for sequential data access and high-volume storage. The h1.8xlarge is suited for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and log processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services h1.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 8000 GB of local HDD storage, designed for cost-effective distributed file systems and data warehousing.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local HDD storage and balanced multi-core performance for large-scale sequential data workloads.

8000 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity sequential data access
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a Xen hypervisor
